name: codex-integrity
description: Stop-hook integrity gate, ON by default (adversarial mode): on every non-trivial turn it auto-runs an end-of-turn Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) adversarial review that grounds itself in the actual changed files and BLOCKS on anything flagged, including bugs introduced or left unfixed, discovered frontier work that can cause undesired behavior, and created or preserved tech debt. Fail-open — if the codex CLI is absent/errors/times out it falls back to a do:change-skeptic advisory and never hard-blocks. Opt out with `/do:run codex off` (or `toggle`), which reverts to an advisory reminder only. Requires the codex CLI to perform the review. DISCLOSURE: this module sends the (secret-scrubbed) text of each reviewed turn to an EXTERNAL LLM (the local codex CLI / OpenAI). Turn text is routed through the shared secret-scrubber before egress (fail-closed: if scrubbing fails, nothing is sent). By DEFAULT codex runs WORKSPACE-WRITE and will EDIT the repository when it judges a fix better; the files it changes are surfaced in the Stop output and an audit log under .claude/state/codex-integrity/ for review before you stop. Set ASK_CODEX_ALLOW_EDITS=0 to force read-only/advise-only (no writes).

disclosure.dataSent: the assistant's turn text for the reviewed turn, secret-scrubbed before egress via lib/do-mon-context.js --scrub (fail-closed: if scrubbing is unavailable, no text is sent and the review degrades to the in-session do:change-skeptic)
disclosure.execution: workspace-write by DEFAULT — codex runs with --sandbox workspace-write and EDITS the repository when it judges a fix better (files it changes are surfaced in the Stop output + an audit log under .claude/state/codex-integrity/). Set ASK_CODEX_ALLOW_EDITS=0 to force read-only/advise-only (--sandbox read-only, no writes). The secret-scrub before egress is unchanged in either mode
